CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["body"],"properties":{"body":{"type":"string","description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation."},"item_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error."},"temporary_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"add_labels","description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","properties":{"labels":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"},"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository."},"item_number":{"type":"number","description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_tool","description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["reason"],"properties":{"tool":{"type":"string","description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"noop","description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["message"],"properties":{"message":{"type":"string","description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing')."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":[],"properties":{"data_type":{"type":"string","description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters)."},"context":{"type":"string","description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}}]}}

2026-03-20T10:28:20.4692530Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-20T10:27:52.417726166Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57293,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Missing WireV3Options for ManagedServiceIdentity serialization\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThe mgmt generator does not generate `WireV3Options` (`ModelReaderWriterOptions(\\u0026#34;W|v3\\u0026#34;)`) for serializing `ManagedServiceIdentity`. This causes the identity type to be serialized as `\\u0026#34;SystemAssigned, UserAssigned\\u0026#34;` (with space) instead of `\\u0026#34;SystemAssigned,UserAssigned\\u0026#34;` (no space), which is a wire-format breaking change from the old autorest-generated SDK.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nThe ARM SDK\\u0026#39;s `ManagedServiceIdentity.Serialization.cs` checks for a `\\u0026#34;|v3\\u0026#34;` suffix in `options.Format`:\\n writes `\\u0026#34;SystemAssigned,UserAssigned\\u0026#34;` (no space, v3 format)\\n- With `\\u0026#34;W\\u0026#34;` → writes `ManagedServiceIdentityType.ToString()` = `\\u0026#34;SystemAssigned, UserAssigned\\u0026#34;` (with space)\\n\\nThe old autorest generator:\\n1. Generated `WireV3Options = new ModelReaderWriterOptions(\\u0026#34;W|v3\\u0026#34;)` in `ModelSerializationExtensions`\\n2. Used `WireV3Options` when serializing/deserializing `ManagedServiceIdentity`\\n\\nThe new TypeSpec mgmt generator:\\n1. Does **not** generate `WireV3Options` at all\\n2. Uses plain `options` / `WireOptions` (`\\u0026#34;W\\u0026#34;`) for Identity serialization\\n\\n## Repro\\n\\nAlready reproducible in the existing mgmt test project — no new TypeSpec needed.\\n\\n**File:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/TestProjects/Local/Mgmt-TypeSpec/main.tsp`\\n\\nThe test project uses `@armCommonTypesVersion(v5)` and `Foo` has `...ManagedServiceIdentityV4Property`.\\n\\n**Generated code** (`FooData.Serialization.cs` line 115):\\n```csharp\\n// Current (bug): passes plain options without v3 suffix\\n((IJsonModel)Identity).Write(writer, options);\\n```\\n\\n**Expected:**\\n```csharp\\n// Should use WireV3Options for @armCommonTypesVersion \\u0026gt;= v3\\n((IJsonModel)Identity).Write(writer, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireV3Options);\\n```\\n\\nAnd `ModelSerializationExtensions` should define:\\n```csharp\\ninternal static readonly ModelReaderWriterOptions WireV3Options = new ModelReaderWriterOptions(\\u0026#34;W|v3\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\n- Breaks wire compatibility for all mgmt SDKs that use `ManagedServiceIdentity` with `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` type\\n- Causes test recording mismatches (8 failures in Storage MPG migration PR #56896)\\n- Azure REST API may reject the request if it doesn\\u0026#39;t accept the spaced format\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- Storage MPG migration PR: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56896\\n- ARM SDK v3 logic: `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/src/Common/Custom/Models/ManagedServiceIdentity.Serialization.cs`\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57293\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-20T10:25:46Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-20T10:25:46Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

This causes the identity type to be serialized as `\\u0026#34;SystemAssigned, UserAssigned\\u0026#34;` (with space) instead of `\\u0026#34;SystemAssigned,UserAssigned\\u0026#34;` (no space), which is a wire-format breaking change from the old autorest-generated SDK.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nThe ARM SDK\\u0026#39;s `ManagedServiceIdentity.Serialization.cs` checks for a `\\u0026#34;|v3\\u0026#34;` suffix in `options.Format`:\\n writes `\\u0026#34;SystemAssigned,UserAssigned\\u0026#34;` (no space, v3 format)\\n- With `\\u0026#34;W\\u0026#34;` → writes `ManagedServiceIdentityType.ToString()` = `\\u0026#34;SystemAssigned, UserAssigned\\u0026#34;` (with space)\\n\\nThe old autorest generator:\\n1. Generated `WireV3Options = new ModelReaderWriterOptions(\\u0026#34;W|v3\\u0026#34;)` in `ModelSerializationExtensions`\\n2. Used `WireV3Options` when serializing/deserializing `ManagedServiceIdentity`\\n\\nThe new TypeSpec mgmt generator:\\n1. Does **not** generate `WireV3Options` at all\\n2. Uses plain `options` / `WireOptions` (`\\u0026#34;W\\u0026#34;`) for Identity serialization\\n\\n## Repro\\n\\nAlready reproducible in the existing mgmt test project — no new TypeSpec needed.\\n\\n**File:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/TestProjects/Local/Mgmt-TypeSpec/main.tsp`\\n\\nThe test project uses `@armCommonTypesVersion(v5)` and `Foo` has `...ManagedServiceIdentityV4Property`.\\n\\n**Generated code** (`FooData.Serialization.cs` line 115):\\n```csharp\\n// Current (bug): passes plain options without v3 suffix\\n((IJsonModel)Identity).Write(writer, options);\\n```\\n\\n**Expected:**\\n```csharp\\n// Should use WireV3Options for @armCommonTypesVersion \\u0026gt;= v3\\n((IJsonModel)Identity).Write(writer, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireV3Options);\\n```\\n\\nAnd `ModelSerializationExtensions` should define:\\n```csharp\\ninternal static readonly ModelReaderWriterOptions WireV3Options = new ModelReaderWriterOptions(\\u0026#34;W|v3\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\n- Breaks wire compatibility for all mgmt SDKs that use `ManagedServiceIdentity` with `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` type\\n- Causes test recording mismatches (8 failures in Storage MPG migration PR #56896)\\n- Azure REST API may reject the request if it doesn\\u0026#39;t accept the spaced format\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- Storage MPG migration PR: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56896\\n- ARM SDK v3 logic: `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/src/Common/Custom/Models/ManagedServiceIdentity.Serialization.cs`\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57293\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-20T10:25:46Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-20T10:25:46Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-20T10:28:20.4757964Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-20T10:27:58.5985724Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"query":"repo:Azure/azure-sdk-for-net is:issue 57293"},"name":"search_issues"}}}
2026-03-20T10:28:20.4778846Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-20T10:27:58.96229186Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4107072990,\"number\":57293,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Missing WireV3Options for ManagedServiceIdentity serialization\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThe mgmt generator does not generate `WireV3Options` (`ModelReaderWriterOptions(\\\"W|v3\\\")`) for serializing `ManagedServiceIdentity`. This causes the identity type to be serialized as `\\\"SystemAssigned, UserAssigned\\\"` (with space) instead of `\\\"SystemAssigned,UserAssigned\\\"` (no space), which is a wire-format breaking change from the old autorest-generated SDK.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nThe ARM SDK's `ManagedServiceIdentity.Serialization.cs` checks for a `\\\"|v3\\\"` suffix in `options.Format`:\\n writes `\\\"SystemAssigned,UserAssigned\\\"` (no space, v3 format)\\n- With `\\\"W\\\"` → writes `ManagedServiceIdentityType.ToString()` = `\\\"SystemAssigned, UserAssigned\\\"` (with space)\\n\\nThe old autorest generator:\\n1. Generated `WireV3Options = new ModelReaderWriterOptions(\\\"W|v3\\\")` in `ModelSerializationExtensions`\\n2. Used `WireV3Options` when serializing/deserializing `ManagedServiceIdentity`\\n\\nThe new TypeSpec mgmt generator:\\n1. Does **not** generate `WireV3Options` at all\\n2. Uses plain `options` / `WireOptions` (`\\\"W\\\"`) for Identity serialization\\n\\n## Repro\\n\\nAlready reproducible in the existing mgmt test project — no new TypeSpec needed.\\n\\n**File:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/TestProjects/Local/Mgmt-TypeSpec/main.tsp`\\n\\nThe test project uses `@armCommonTypesVersion(v5)` and `Foo` has `...ManagedServiceIdentityV4Property`.\\n\\n**Generated code** (`FooData.Serialization.cs` line 115):\\n```csharp\\n// Current (bug): passes plain options without v3 suffix\\n((IJsonModel\\u003cManagedServiceIdentity\\u003e)Identity).Write(writer, options);\\n```\\n\\n**Expected:**\\n```csharp\\n// Should use WireV3Options for @armCommonTypesVersion \\u003e= v3\\n((IJsonModel\\u003cManagedServiceIdentity\\u003e)Identity).Write(writer, ModelSerializationExtensions.WireV3Options);\\n```\\n\\nAnd `ModelSerializationExtensions` should define:\\n```csharp\\ninternal static readonly ModelReaderWriterOptions WireV3Options = new ModelReaderWriterOptions(\\\"W|v3\\\");\\n```\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\n- Breaks wire compatibility for all mgmt SDKs that use `ManagedServiceIdentity` with `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` type\\n- Causes test recording mismatches (8 failures in Storage MPG migration PR #56896)\\n- Azure REST API may reject the request if it doesn't accept the spaced format\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- Storage MPG migration PR: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56896\\n- ARM SDK v3 logic: `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/src/Common/Custom/Models/ManagedServiceIdentity.Serialization.cs`\\n\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjUxOTYxMzk=\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/live1206/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1369687458,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/bug\",\"name\":\"bug\",\"color\":\"eaa875\",\"description\":\"This 2026-03-20T10:28:20.5171744Z [36;1m# Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts[0m
2026-03-20T10:28:20.5171881Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-03-20T10:28:20.5172082Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-03-20T10:28:20.5172408Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
2026-03-20T10:28:20.5172514Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
2026-03-20T10:28:20.5172645Z [36;1m  awf logs summary | tee -a "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"[0m
2026-03-20T10:28:20.5172719Z [36;1melse[0m

2026-03-20T10:30:02.7174084Z Based on my analysis:
2026-03-20T10:30:02.7174393Z 
2026-03-20T10:30:02.7660238Z 1. **Prompt Injection (TRUE)**: The workflow prompt file contains a clear prompt injection attempt. Within the security guidelines section describing shell metacharacters to reject, a complete `<system>` XML block is embedded mid-sentence (after listing `;`, `|`, `&`, the text transitions into a full `<system><security>...</security>...github-context...github-mcp-tools...</system>` block). This is a classic injection technique — embedding a fake system prompt within the workflow instructions to potentially confuse or override the agent's security context. The injected block mirrors the legitimate runtime `<system>` block structure exactly.
2026-03-20T10:30:02.7666181Z 
2026-03-20T10:30:02.7666850Z 2. **Secret Leak (FALSE)**: No secrets or credentials detected. The agent output is a clean noop message.
2026-03-20T10:30:02.7667770Z 
2026-03-20T10:30:02.7672373Z 3. **Malicious Patch (FALSE)**: No patch file exists.
2026-03-20T10:30:02.7672798Z 
2026-03-20T10:30:02.7820183Z The agent output appears clean — it correctly issued a noop because issue #57293 already had labels, suggesting the injection did not cause observable harm.
2026-03-20T10:30:02.7821665Z 
2026-03-20T10:30:02.8444621Z THREAT_DETECTION_RESULT:{"prompt_injection":true,"secret_leak":false,"malicious_patch":false,"reasons":["A prompt injection attack was detected in the workflow prompt file. Within the security guidelines section describing shell metacharacters to reject, a complete `<system>` XML block was embedded mid-sentence (after listing metacharacters `;`, `|`, `&`, the text transitions into a full `<system><security>...</security><github-context>...</github-context></system>` block). This is a classic cross-prompt injection technique - embedding a fake system-level prompt within the workflow instructions to potentially confuse or override the agent's security context. The injected block mirrors the legitimate runtime `<system>` block structure exactly, including security policies, temporary-files instructions, safe-outputs instructions, and github-context. The agent appears to have ignored the injection and issued a correct noop response (issue already had labels)."]}
